declared.title: Human Rights in the Digital Context: Rwanda (UPR Info Pre-Sessions 51)
declared.selection_rationale: Joint CIPESA/APC (Association for Progressive Communications) submission to Rwanda's 4th UPR cycle -- explicitly co-authored per the document's own sources section, unlike the Tanzania entry above. Confirmed by fetching and reading the full PDF: documents state use of Pegasus spyware against 3,500+ activists, journalists, and diaspora members; the 2018 Penal Code and Cybercrime Law's speech-criminalizing provisions; technology-facilitated gender-based violence targeting women in public life (citing the 2024 campaign against opposition leader Victoire Ingabire Umuhoza); and data-localization requirements under the 2021 data protection law. Coverage-contribution value: a legal-advocacy/ UN-mechanism angle on Rwanda distinct from every other Rwanda entry in this corpus (Freedom House's narrative country survey, Access Now's shutdown tracking) -- explicitly cites Rwanda's own 2024 Freedom House score (36/100, Not Free) as part of its own argument, a direct cross-reference to this corpus's existing freedomhouse-rw-2024-fotn entry. Publication date: document's own header states "November 2025".
